title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: R package provides interface GPfit package mlr3 ecosystem. implements Gaussian Process regression automatic feature scaling tunable hyperparameters. Note: package implements GPfit (pure R) instead GPyTorch (Python) suggested issue #487. GPfit chosen avoid Python dependencies (reticulate) provide pure R implementation easier maintain deploy.

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: site can found : https://mlr3learners-gpfit.netlify.app/ site includes API references, usage guides detailed performance benchmarks.

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: install , can use command:

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: package provides regr.gpfit learner mlr3. Features automatically scaled [0,1] required GPfit.

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
code: library(mlr3) library(mlr3learners.gpfit)  # Create regression task (exclude factor columns) iris_numeric = iris[, c("Sepal.Length", "Sepal.Width", "Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")] task = as_task_regr(iris_numeric, target = "Sepal.Length", id = "iris_sepal")  # Create and train learner learner = lrn("regr.gpfit") learner$train(task)  # Make predictions prediction = learner$predict(task) prediction$score(msr("regr.mse"))

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: Available hyperparameters: - corr_type: Correlation function type (ŌĆ£exponentialŌĆØ ŌĆ£maternŌĆØ) - corr_power: Power parameter exponential correlation (default: 1.95) - corr_nu: Smoothness parameter Mat├®rn correlation (default: 2.5)
code: # Mat├®rn correlation with custom smoothness learner = lrn("regr.gpfit") learner$param_set$values = list(corr_type = "matern", corr_nu = 1.5) learner$train(task)  # Exponential correlation with custom power learner = lrn("regr.gpfit") learner$param_set$values = list(corr_type = "exponential", corr_power = 1.8) learner$train(task)

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: Performance evaluated using Mean Squared Error (MSE). Results based 5-fold cross-validation. Note: Lower values indicate better performance. Bold values represent best learner task.

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: GPfit learn non-trivial patterns? - iris_sepal: 85.38% improvement baseline - iris_petal: 97.76% improvement baseline YES, GPfit learns non-trivial patterns GPfit competitive algorithms? - iris_sepal: GPfit ranks #1 4 algorithms - iris_petal: GPfit ranks #1 4 algorithms GPfit achieves best performance datasets complete benchmark analysis available benchmark.R file.

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: Automatic feature scaling [0,1] correctly applied training prediction. learner stores scaling parameters (.x_min, .x_max) training reuses consistent normalization prediction time.

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: iris_sepal: iris_petal: Key observations: - MSE Train < MSE Test ŌåÆ Proper scaling overfitting - MSE Test << MSE Baseline ŌåÆ Excellent generalization - Improvement > 80% datasets - Hyperparameters allow fine-tuning performance

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: Automatic feature scaling: Features automatically normalized [0,1] required GPfit Numerical stability: GPfit uses ŌĆśnuggetŌĆÖ parameter stability (MSE Train ŌēĀ 0, prevents overfitting) Tunable hyperparameters: Choice exponential Mat├®rn correlation adjustable parameters

title: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3
text: package includes: 9 unit tests (100% passing) Complete validation prediction quality Comparative benchmark 3 algorithms Continuous integration via GitHub Actions Code coverage tracked via Codecov Documentation website deployed Netlify Automatic feature scaling validation Tunable hyperparameters (corr_type, corr_power, corr_nu)

title: mlr3learners.gpfit: Gaussian Process Learner for mlr3 ŌĆö mlr3learners.gpfit-package
text: Implements Gaussian Process regression using GPfit package mlr3 learner. Provides GP_fit() functionality within mlr3 framework machine learning tasks.

what: Gaussian Process Regression Learner ŌĆö mlr_learners_regr.gpfit
title: Gaussian Process Regression Learner ŌĆö mlr_learners_regr.gpfit
text: Gaussian Process regression using GPfit package. Calls GPfit::GP_fit() training predict method predictions. Features automatically scaled unit hypercube required GPfit.

title: Gaussian Process Regression Learner ŌĆö mlr_learners_regr.gpfit
text: corr_type (character(1)) Correlation function type: "exponential" (default) "matern". corr_power (numeric(1)) Power parameter exponential correlation. Default: 1.95. corr_nu (numeric(1)) Smoothness parameter Matern correlation. Default: 2.5.

title: Gaussian Process Regression Learner ŌĆö mlr_learners_regr.gpfit
code: library(mlr3)  # Create a regression task task = tsk("mtcars")  # Create the learner with default parameters learner = lrn("regr.gpfit") learner$train(task) learner$predict(task) #>  #> ŌöĆŌöĆ <PredictionRegr> for 32 observations: ŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆŌöĆ #>  row_ids truth response #>        1  21.0     21.0 #>        2  21.0     21.0 #>        3  22.8     22.8 #>      ---   ---      --- #>       30  19.7     19.7 #>       31  15.0     15.0 #>       32  21.4     21.4  # Use Matern correlation learner2 = lrn("regr.gpfit") learner2$param_set$values = list(corr_type = "matern", corr_nu = 1.5) learner2$train(task)
